steps.title: Convert the arc to a fraction
steps.content:
The arc is $45^\circ$ out of the full $360^\circ$ circle:

$$\frac{45}{360}=\frac{1}{8}$$

So the arc length is $\frac{1}{8}$ of the circumference.

steps.title: Scale up to the full circle
steps.content:
The arc length is $5$ inches, so multiply by $8$ to get the full circumference:

$$5\cdot 8=40$$

The answer is D.
